title: Minimum Rooms to Host All Meetings
description:
Given a list of meeting time intervals [start, end), return the minimum number of conference rooms required to host all meetings without overlap. Intervals are half-open so a meeting ending at t frees a room for a meeting starting at t.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
- -10^9 ≤ start < end ≤ 10^9
explanation:
Intuition
The peak number of concurrent meetings equals the required rooms. One way is to track a running count with a sweep-line: convert intervals into +1 at start and -1 at end, sort by time with ends before starts on ties, then accumulate. Alternatively, use a min-heap of end times and compare starts to earliest end.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Extract and sort all boundary events (start as +1, end as -1)
2. Main Algorithm: Scan events in order, updating active count and tracking the maximum
3. Return Result: Return the maximum active count

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n log n) due to sorting
- Space Complexity: O(1) beyond event list

Key Insights
- Tie-break by processing end before start at same time
- Half-open semantics allow sharing a room at equal times

Common Pitfalls
- If you process starts before ends, counts inflate
- Min-heap solution is fine but sweep-line is simpler to type
hints: Sort ends before starts on tie
